steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that 'Mère Poussepin' marks a significant change in Gwen John's artistic development. Supporting evidence must show a before-and-after shift in her own style — not external opinion or influence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'critical interpretation': Describes how critics read the painting, not a change in John's technique or approach. ✗
  • Choice B — 'overall achievement': Speaks to the painting's place in her career as a whole, but does not establish a stylistic before-and-after. ✗
  • Choice C — 'earlier style she never returned to': Directly states that her prior paintings belonged to an earlier style she permanently abandoned, proving the claimed shift in artistic development. ✓
  • Choice D — 'external influence': Credits outside forces rather than demonstrating an actual change in her own work. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
